title: Star Reflection Basis Resolution and Degradation Policy

time_representation.birth_minute_interval: [local HH:MM:00, local HH:MM plus one minute)

prerequisites: runtime response and privacy state valid
current Nakshatra name and index valid
previous and next transition objects complete
birth local minute maps uniquely in the resolved timezone
exact_session_only_resolution.resolved_current_segment: The complete birth-minute interval lies at or after the previous transition and strictly before the next transition; use the current sunrise-anchored Nakshatra identity because the transitions prove that the same segment covers the entire birth minute.
exact_session_only_resolution.boundary_minute: If either transition occurs inside the birth-minute interval, do not choose either side; return exact_transition_minute_ambiguous.
exact_session_only_resolution.before_previous_transition: Return exact_outside_single_transition_window; the runtime does not provide the earlier transition needed to prove the birth segment.
exact_session_only_resolution.at_or_after_next_transition: Return exact_outside_single_transition_window unless a later governed runtime contract supplies the next-next boundary. The available next.toIndex remains transition metadata and must not be extended across an unproven interval.
exact_session_only_resolution.next_to_index_use: May identify the segment beginning exactly at the governed next transition, but current HH:MM minute precision cannot claim that segment when the transition falls inside the entered minute or when no next-next boundary proves the remainder of the interval.

unknown_resolution.allowed_context: The sunrise Nakshatra may be shown only as day-level Panchang context, explicitly not as the user's birth star.

universal_rule: No unresolved, unavailable or ambiguous state may be converted into a fabricated Panchang value, birth Nakshatra or prediction.
